metadata.summary: GCP service overview, gcloud CLI setup, authentication, configuration management, CI/CD deployment patterns, disaster recovery, and cost optimization commands.

content.title: GCP Infrastructure Overview and CLI
content.summary: Google Cloud Platform infrastructure best practices (2025-2026): service catalogue, gcloud CLI setup, authentication, named configurations, CI/CD deployment patterns, disaster recovery, and cost optimization using Recommender and BigQuery billing exports.

why.p: GCP spans compute, serverless, networking, IAM, storage, and operations services. A consistent CLI setup and named configuration profiles eliminate environment drift. Deployment patterns (Cloud Build → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run / GKE) give repeatable, auditable rollouts. Recommender API surfaces idle resources and over-granted permissions automatically.

list.item: Setting up a new GCP project or onboarding a developer to an existing project.
Establishing CI/CD pipelines that build, push, and deploy container images.
Selecting the right GCP service for a given workload type (VM vs serverless vs GKE).
Running cost audits or finding idle resources via Recommender.
Configuring disaster recovery across regions for Compute Engine or Cloud SQL.
Deep IAM / network hardening — use gcp-security-iam and gcp-networking-vpc instead.
Terraform IaC templates — use gcp-terraform-templates instead.
LLM prompt generation for GCP tasks — use gcp-llm-prompts instead.

section.name: service-catalogue
section.p: GCP service categories and canonical service selection:

list.item: Compute: Compute Engine (VMs), GKE (Kubernetes), Managed Instance Groups (autoscaling).
Serverless: Cloud Run (containers), Cloud Functions Gen2 (event-driven).
Networking: VPC, Cloud NAT, Cloud Load Balancing, Cloud Armor.
IAM: Roles, Service Accounts, Workload Identity.
Storage: Cloud Storage (object), Cloud SQL (relational), BigQuery (analytics).
Operations: Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring, Secret Manager.

rule.statement: Select the compute tier before any infrastructure work: persistent VMs → Compute Engine; managed Kubernetes → GKE Autopilot; containerized stateless apps → Cloud Run; event-driven functions → Cloud Functions Gen2.
rule.rationale: Wrong service selection causes migration debt. Autopilot is the correct default for new Kubernetes workloads; Cloud Run for stateless containers avoids cluster overhead entirely.

section.name: shared-responsibility
section.p: Google manages physical infrastructure, host OS/hypervisor, default encryption, network infrastructure, and global data centers. The customer manages IAM configuration, data classification, application security, workload configuration, and service account keys (or their elimination via Workload Identity).

example.title: Install via apt (Debian/Ubuntu)
example.code:
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/cloud.google.gpg] https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt cloud-sdk main" | \ sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list.d/google-cloud-sdk.list curl https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | \ sudo apt-key --keyring /usr/share/keyrings/cloud.google.gpg add - sudo apt update && sudo apt install google-cloud-cli
# Verify gcloud version

example.title: Authentication modes
example.code:
# Interactive login (human) gcloud auth login
# Service account (CI/CD) gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file=service-account.json
# Application default credentials (local dev) gcloud auth application-default login
# List accounts gcloud auth list

example.title: Named configurations
example.code:
# Set defaults gcloud config set project my-project-id gcloud config set compute/region us-central1 gcloud config set compute/zone us-central1-a
# Named configurations (one per environment) gcloud config configurations create production gcloud config configurations activate production gcloud config configurations list
# View current gcloud config list

example.title: Environment variables for CI/CD
example.code: export CLOUDSDK_CORE_PROJECT="my-project-id" export CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_REGION="us-central1" export CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_ZONE="us-central1-a" export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS="/path/to/service-account.json" export CLOUDSDK_CORE_DISABLE_PROMPTS=1 # non-interactive CI

rule.statement: Use named gcloud configurations (one per environment: dev, staging, prod) rather than repeatedly running gcloud config set. Store CLOUDSDK_CORE_PROJECT and region in environment variables for CI/CD pipelines.
rule.rationale: Named configurations prevent accidental cross-environment commands. Environment variables allow the same pipeline script to target different projects without code changes.

example.title: CI/CD pipeline: build → push → deploy
example.code:
# Build and push to Artifact Registry gcloud builds submit \ --tag us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/my-repo/my-app:$TAG
# Deploy to Cloud Run gcloud run deploy my-app \ --image=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/my-repo/my-app:$TAG \ --region=us-central1 \ --service-account=my-app@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
# Deploy to GKE kubectl set image deployment/my-app \ my-app=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/my-repo/my-app:$TAG

example.title: Disaster recovery: cross-region replicas and snapshots
example.code:
# Cross-region Cloud SQL replica gcloud sql instances create my-replica \ --master-instance-name=my-primary \ --region=europe-west1
# Cross-region snapshot gcloud compute snapshots create my-snapshot-dr \ --source-disk=my-disk \ --source-disk-zone=us-central1-a \ --storage-location=europe-west1
# Multi-region storage bucket gcloud storage buckets create gs://my-dr-bucket --location=eu

example.title: Recommender: idle resources and IAM cleanup
example.code:
# Find idle Compute Engine instances gcloud recommender recommendations list \ --project=my-project \ --location=us-central1-a \ --recommender=google.compute.instance.IdleResourceRecommender
# IAM Recommender (remove unused permissions) gcloud recommender recommendations list \ --project=my-project \ --location=global \ --recommender=google.iam.policy.Recommender
# List committed use discounts gcloud compute commitments list

example.title: BigQuery billing analysis — top 10 services by cost (30 days)
example.code: bq query --use_legacy_sql=false ' SELECT service.description, SUM(cost) as total_cost FROM `my-project.billing_export.gcp_billing_export_v1_*` WHERE DATE(_PARTITIONTIME) >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10'

rule.statement: Run Recommender weekly for idle resources and IAM over-grants. Export billing data to BigQuery and query monthly. Review committed use discounts quarterly.
rule.rationale: GCP Recommender automates the otherwise manual process of finding waste. BigQuery billing exports give cost attribution by service, label, and project that the Cloud Console cost view aggregates away.
